opp.name: SATS_S2P
opp.next_step: 75% Confidence 1st Jul- still waiting on RFP- coming to Horizon- 4 people 22nd Jun- RFP coming in 2 weeks- agreed to send 4 to Horizon ! Jun 6th Carl to reach out and requalify- invited to Horizon SEA May 11: Met Pierre last week (he definitely enjoys I2O). RFP's taking time as WW scope + finishing data consolidation. Will participate at Horizon. Apr 27: Meeting with operation HoP: RFP is coming. Zycus well positioned with AI-Roadmap and Customers reference. Challenge is around timeline (RFP still expected mid year) Apr 13 Meeting with project leader by end of Apr Mar 27 Some delays due to larger scope evaluation. Meeting with PM by April 20 Mar 20 meeting with sourcing manager postponed after Easter Mar 05- Meeting with IT Procurement director. WW-Project scope (Except SG, Ariba until 2030). Part of larger Back-office transformation. Strong AI and I2O driver initiative. Still some homework before launching RFI. Zycus as one of the most preferred solutions. Feb 05th Meeting with Head of sourcing confirmed by March 04. Currently involved with EY on digital transformation initiative in back office, ending in March. Then, RFP should come Jan 05th: No update on RFP. Risk to move to Q1. Dec 08th Some internal challenge to get budget approval. RFP now expected by mid Jan Nov 24th: Additional questions last week. Prospect working on RFP content. Nov 17th PM confirms RFP to come in before mid Dec. 07th Nov: CPO expects to send RFP by end of 2025. Good engagement so far. 30th Oct- Whilst moving this opp to Formal Eval- note that we have NOT received the RFP as was communicated- expected in next 1-2 weeks 13rd Oct: RFP expected by end of Oct 26th Sep: Carl expected to meet CPO by mid Oct to focus our local momentum. 17th Sep: RFI expected by end Oct. Demo use cases in Nov. RFP in Dec. Intel says we are well positioned based on ST Eng recent win (both Temasek companies). 08th Sep: Meeting with PM to update on next step on Sep 08 18th Aug: Meeting on Aug 20 during Zycus AI Proc World event. Jul 31: Meeting with CPO in recent local event. Confirms they will engage with Zycus as part of S2P process of selection. Expect 1st engagement by mid Aug
opp.ais_why:
• Per the Apr 27 2026 rep note from Guillaume Pasquet: 'Meeting with operation HoP: RFP is coming. Zycus well positioned with AI-Roadmap and Customers reference. Challenge is around timeline (RFP still expected mid year).' Per Mar 05 2026: 'Meeting with IT Procurement director. WW-Project scope (Except SG, Ariba until 2030). Part of larger Back-office transformation. Strong AI and I2O driver initiative. Still some homework before launching RFI. Zycus as one of the most preferred solutions.' Buyer side is naming AI explicitly as a driver of the transformation.
• Per the May 11 2026 rep note: 'Met Pierre last week (he definitely enjoys I2O). RFP's taking time as WW scope + finishing data consolidation. Will participate at Horizon.' Pierre is named (champion at SATS) and is documented as enthusiastic about I2O (Intake-to-Order, Zycus' agentic flow). He's also planning to attend Horizon (Zycus customer event) - signal of advocacy.
• High confidence: Product list is highly AI-loaded (Merlin Intake + Agentic AI + ANA), NextStep is exceptionally rich across 12+ dated entries spanning Oct 2025-May 2026 with consistent AI-themed language ('Strong AI and I2O driver', 'AI-Roadmap'). Single Avoma meeting was not_recorded but rep notes establish Pierre as champion. Stage = Qualified but 75% confidence reported by rep.

items.quote: Some internal challenge to get budget approval
Part of larger Back-office transformation. Strong AI and I2O driver initiative

items.area: No recorded buyer call: voice-of-customer evidence is rep-logged notes and emails only

metrics.narrative: The buyer has a strong qualitative case but no quantified financial target. The 05 Mar 2026 Next Step frames the deal as 'Part of larger Back-office transformation. Strong AI and I2O driver initiative', positioning AI-powered procurement (Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, I2O) as the primary driver, and the 27 Apr 2026 note confirms Zycus is 'well positioned with AI-Roadmap and Customers reference'. No ROI model, savings target, cycle-time or headcount figure exists in any source, and the 08 Dec 2025 note flags 'Some internal challenge to get budget approval', which implies the absence of a hard business case that would defend a $350k worldwide investment at the approval gate. The move is to co-build a quantified savings model with procurement leadership before commercials.

champion.narrative: Pierre Vignaud (Head of Airport Services Sourcing and Procurement) is the developing champion: he is the most engaged stakeholder, enthusiastic about I2O (11 May 2026: 'Met Pierre last week, he definitely enjoys I2O'), and is actively pulling Zycus in, requesting Horizon invitations for himself and the team who will run the S2P RFX (19 Jun 2026 emails). What has developed is genuine advocacy behavior (organizing his team around a Zycus event, championing the AI story). What is still missing is evidence that he has introduced Zycus to the economic buyer, secured budget, or differentiated Zycus against competitors internally. Elaine Chen (VP, Decision Maker) is engaged on logistics but not yet a champion.

competition.narrative: Three named rivals sit in the Competitors field: Coupa, GEP Worldwide, and SAP Ariba. SAP Ariba is the confirmed Singapore incumbent, contracted until 2030, which is why Singapore is excluded from this worldwide scope (05 Mar 2026), so its threat is a potential global-consolidation pitch rather than a live head-to-head. GEP and Coupa are logged in the field but carry no call, email, or Next Step activity, so their engagement level is unknown. Zycus is buyer-stated as 'one of the most preferred solutions' (05 Mar 2026) and 'well positioned' on AI roadmap and references (27 Apr 2026), and the ST Engineering Temasek reference is a proven wedge (17 Sep 2025). We win by shaping the RFP around AI/I2O and reference credibility before competitors mobilize.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is a worldwide back-office and source-to-pay transformation with AI/I2O modernization as the explicit ambition (05 Mar 2026: 'Part of larger Back-office transformation. Strong AI and I2O driver initiative'), running alongside an EY-led digital transformation of the back office that was set to end in March (05 Feb 2026). This is confirmed strategic pain articulated by procurement leadership, with Pierre Vignaud (Head of Airport Services Sourcing and Procurement) as the operational pain owner who 'definitely enjoys I2O' (11 May 2026). What is not yet articulated is the cost of inaction in numbers, so the pain is real and named but not quantified.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have been discussed: there is no MSA/SOW activity, no legal, procurement-channel, or InfoSec conversation on record, which is expected at Qualified stage pre-RFP. The one dated forcing function on the paper side is that Ariba is contracted in Singapore until 2030, which is why Singapore is carved out of this worldwide deal rather than a lever on it. Visibility here will only open once the RFP defines the commercial and legal track; the move is to ask the buyer for the intended procurement and legal timeline when the RFP issues.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is named in the contact roles. Elaine Chen (VP, Global Procurement) is the primary Decision Maker, and a CPO is referenced but unnamed (07 Nov 2025 Next Step: 'CPO expects to send RFP by end of 2025'; 26 Sep 2025: 'Carl expected to meet CPO by mid Oct'). The 08 Dec 2025 note 'Some internal challenge to get budget approval' shows budget authority sits above the working team and is not yet mapped. What the evidence does show is that Elaine Chen is the senior procurement decision-maker of record and the CPO controls the RFP release, so mapping the CPO and confirming who signs the $350k is the ranked move.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a structured, federated worldwide S2P selection: a formal RFI then RFP, driven out of global procurement with a worldwide scope that excludes Singapore (where Ariba is locked until 2030). The process has repeatedly slipped as the buyer completes data consolidation and internal alignment (11 May 2026: 'RFP's taking time as WW scope + finishing data consolidation'). Pierre's 19 Jun 2026 email states the team plans 'to launch our S2P RFX Process' and 'to launch within the next 3 months', naming the two people who will run the RFI and RFQ. The approval chain above the working team, including budget sign-off, is not yet documented.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are emerging but not yet formalized because the RFP has not issued. The buyer has consistently signalled AI and Intake-to-Order capability as the lead criterion (05 Mar 2026: 'Strong AI and I2O driver initiative'; 27 Apr 2026: Zycus positioned 'with AI-Roadmap and Customers reference'), alongside worldwide-deployment fit (WW scope excluding Singapore) and reference credibility (17 Sep 2025: 'well positioned based on ST Eng recent win, both Temasek companies'). These are inferred from rep-logged buyer conversations rather than a published requirement document; the formal criteria will crystallize in the RFP, which is the artifact to secure early to shape them toward Zycus differentiation.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Buyer is leaning our way — 'Zycus as one of the most preferred solutions' (05 Mar 2026) and 'well positioned with AI-Roadmap and Customers reference' (27 Apr 2026); champion Pierre 'definitely enjoys I2O' (11 May 2026).
bullets.text: Buyer is leaning our way — 'Zycus as one of the most preferred solutions' (05 Mar 2026) and 'well positioned with AI-Roadmap and Customers reference' (27 Apr 2026); champion Pierre…

bullets.full: A competitor is pushing back — Zycus is buyer-stated as 'one of the most preferred solutions' (05 Mar 2026) for the worldwide S2P scope. SAP Ariba is the Singapore incumbent locked until 2030 and therefore carved out of scope, so its only real threat is a global-consolidation counter-pitch; GEP Worldwide and Coupa are named in the CRM but show no activity in any call, email, or Next Step and read as passive shortlist presence. The strongest current dynamic is not a rival winning but the risk of an Ariba 'expand us globally' pitch and the absence of a hardened Zycus business case; no competitor is buyer-preferred.

blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.
blocks.title: Deal momentum

bullets.text: Live next steps with named dates

blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Zycus is buyer-stated as 'one of the most preferred solutions' (05 Mar 2026) for the worldwide S2P scope.

bullets.text: RFP promised repeatedly since Oct 2025 and still not received; close date pushed twice and now mathematically unachievable on current pace.
Internal budget-approval challenge flagged with no quantified ROI to defend the $350k.
Economic buyer / CPO who owns budget is unnamed and unmapped; Decision Maker Elaine Chen engaged only on logistics.
SAP Ariba incumbency in Singapore (until 2030) could seed a global-consolidation counter-pitch across the worldwide scope.

items: Ask Pierre to confirm the RFP release date in writing and share the draft scope. (by 10 Jul)
Book a session with Elaine Chen to map the budget owner and worldwide decision process. (by 17 Jul)
Run an AI-roadmap and reference working session for the 4 SATS attendees at Horizon SEA. (by 22 Jul)
blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.win_position: Win 21 is carried by preference strength +1.00 (weight 20); momentum 59 vs stage-expected 50. Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 59 (moving forward): Next Step carries 12 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 14 is anchored on the stage win (21), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 59, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: customer requested next meeting (from call evidence).
Verdict flags close-date risk this sweep.

No recorded call to test over/under-rating, but rep notes and product scope consistently confirm high AI appetite.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative strategic pain (worldwide back-office / AI-I2O transformation) is clearly articulated, but no ROI, savings, or cycle-time figure has been quantified, and budget approval was flagged as an internal challenge (08 Dec 2025).

deal_movement.summary: Warm, actively engaged deal held back by a chronically slipping RFP. Ownership moved to Carl Kimball on 06 Jun 2026; the close date has been pushed twice; the buyer confirmed 4 attendees for Horizon SEA and is pulling for more invitations, but the RFP promised for early Jul has not arrived.

RFP overdue against the buyer's own '2 weeks' commitment (22 Jun 2026) signals evaluation-timeline drift, not relationship cooling.
confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is currently warm and rep-side plus buyer-side active (buyer requesting Horizon invitations, 4 confirmed attendees), but momentum on the actual evaluation is stalled behind the unreleased RFP.

north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 70 (11 Sep 2026 from 03 Jul). Time in Qualified stage: 338 days since creation on 30 Jul 2025, 289 days since qualified on 17 Sep 2025. RFP promised Oct 2025, then Dec 2025, then mid-Jan 2026, then mid-year 2026, most recently 22 Jun 'in 2 weeks' (due ~06 Jul). CloseDate already pushed 15 May -> 15 Jun -> 11 Sep 2026. Minimum path from RFP receipt: response 2 wks + shortlist 2 wks + demos/workshops 3 wks + commercials 2 wks + negotiation/legal 2 wks = 11+ weeks; with no RFP received and 70 days of runway there is no buffer for a worldwide, federated, multi-stakeholder evaluation.
evidence: Next Step 01 Jul 2026: '75% Confidence 1st Jul- still waiting on RFP- coming to Horizon- 4 people' confirms RFP not yet received and 4 SATS attendees confirmed for Horizon SEA.
Next Step 22 Jun 2026: 'RFP coming in 2 weeks- agreed to send 4 to Horizon !' set the RFP promise for ~06 Jul.
Field history 07 Apr 2026: CloseDate moved 15 Jun 2026 -> 11 Sep 2026, the second push of the date.

Next Step 05 Mar 2026: 'WW-Project scope (Except SG, Ariba until 2030). Part of larger Back-office transformation. Strong AI and I2O driver initiative... Zycus as one of the most preferred solutions.' confirms WW scope, Ariba locked in Singapore until 2030, and Zycus preferred.
Next Step 08 Dec 2025: 'Some internal challenge to get budget approval. RFP now expected by mid Jan' flags a live budget-approval risk.
LastActivityDate 30 Jun 2026 plus buyer emails through 19 Jun 2026 (Pierre and Elaine registering for Horizon) confirm active, warm engagement.
north_star_verdict.headline: Engaged, well-positioned deal with strong AI appetite, but the 11 Sep close date is not achievable: the RFP promised on 22 Jun 'in 2 weeks' is overdue and no RFP is yet in hand, so the date will slip to Q4 2026 or Q1 2027.

flags: Turn Horizon SEA (4 SATS attendees, 21-22 Jul) into a working AI-roadmap and reference session, not just attendance, to lock Zycus differentiation before the RFP.
Map and engage the economic buyer / CPO who owns budget approval; the working team is engaged but no budget owner is confirmed for the $350k.
Secure the draft RFP requirements early via Pierre to shape evaluation criteria toward AI/I2O and reference credibility before GEP, Coupa, or Ariba mobilize.
best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers for a warm, well-positioned but pre-RFP worldwide deal whose date is at risk and whose power map and business case have holes.

customer_preference.evidence: 'Zycus as one of the most preferred solutions' (05 Mar 2026) and 'well positioned with AI-Roadmap and Customers reference' (27 Apr 2026); champion Pierre 'definitely enjoys I2O' (11 May 2026).

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is buyer-stated as 'one of the most preferred solutions' (05 Mar 2026) for the worldwide S2P scope. SAP Ariba is the Singapore incumbent locked until 2030 and therefore carved out of scope, so its only real threat is a global-consolidation counter-pitch; GEP Worldwide and Coupa are named in the CRM but show no activity in any call, email, or Next Step and read as passive shortlist presence. The strongest current dynamic is not a rival winning but the risk of an Ariba 'expand us globally' pitch and the absence of a hardened Zycus business case; no competitor is buyer-preferred.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.how_we_win: Frame dual-vendor (Ariba Singapore, Zycus worldwide) as a best-of-breed AI strategy; preempt an SAP global-consolidation pitch with the Merlin I2O agentic story and the ST Engineering Temasek reference.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.how_we_win: No activity recorded; likely passive shortlist entry. Defend on AI-driven ROI and reference proof; if GEP activates, position its offering as legacy against Merlin agentic negotiation and I2O.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.how_we_win: No activity recorded; likely passive shortlist entry. If Coupa activates, position agentic AI (Merlin I2O) as next-gen versus traditional workflow automation, with the ST Engineering Temasek reference as enterprise-scale proof.

The narrative is landing; the task is to convert appetite into RFP criteria and a quantified value case.

Zycus aligns strongly on the AI and reference criteria; the exposure is a not-yet-quantified savings case and an unreleased RFP that has not fixed the criteria.
